_strategy: R10: high anomaly rate (4 ns=null, 2 env-fail, 0 valid +1 from 5 in flight). Suggests backend allocator/Redis racing under burst submission. R11 strategy: (1) Drop bursting; submit fewer (K=6) candidates with longer inter-submit sleep. (2) Focus on TWO chaos types only: PodFailure on currency (most-stable winner), PodFailure on image-provider (50% baseline). (3) Use K=6 = 3 currency + 3 image-provider for clean A/B. (4) Save submit_dual.py inter-submit sleep increase for next iteration if needed.
_results: Only z05/z06 image-provider produced traces. z05 → no_anomaly -1. z06 still running. 4/6 (z01-z04) ns=None — backend allocator racing badly when bursting same-app candidates. Conclusion: otel-demo backend cannot handle K=3 same-app within 6s window without ns=None.
